Alright, well welcome back to the training. We are now in module two. I'd just like to take a second to congratulate you for making it through module one. I know it was loaded with a ton of new big concepts and paradigm shifts and just a new approach for writing your sales messaging. I do want to congratulate you on making it through that initial big hurdle. Hopefully your brain cells have had a chance to decompress and really absorb everything that we presented in that one. But acknowledging that a conversion is nothing but the transformation between point A and point B, which was a major premise in module one. In module two, we're actually going to start mapping out that transformation and particularly how it looks across your funnel. So here is how module two is going to break down. In lesson one, you are going to get an introduction to the buyer's journey. You'll see an overview of it. And you know, this is really what we mean by that transformation between point A and point B. You'll also see the seven key milestones that need to be achieved over the course of that journey and how the five sub sequences work together as a vehicle to deliver it. However, before we can really expect a buyer to embark on a journey, it kind of needs to be brought to life. Makes sense, right? A static, a lifeless 2D avatar can't really move. So that's why in lesson two, you are going to get introduced to the three-dimensional, ever-evolving avatar. That sounds like a mouthful, that's okay. You know, we really do want to add all these aspects and senses to our avatar. Give them some life, breathe some life into your avatar. So this is going to give you a chance to really go beyond traditional empathy mapping. Make them feel like a real living, breathing human, which they are. That's kind of the mind-blowing concept. They are a living, breathing, feeling human. And acknowledging that will really start giving you the keys to engineer those aha moments, you know, and that build trust and allow you the right to lead them down that journey. So in this module, you will get the 47 Golden Questions workbook that we alluded to earlier as well as two other really powerful exercises that I turn to every time I start a new project with a client. And whenever I do get blocked, I always turn back to these two exercises. These two exercises alone tend to really unlock a lot of insights, a lot of hooks for emails and messages. And I really encourage you to spend the time on these two exercises as well. So you're going to get introduced to that in lesson two. In lesson three, we're actually going to travel all the way to the other end of the journey and look at what a buyer really is. That's like a pretty basic and even stupid question, you know, what is a buyer? But once you unpack it, you know, actually looking at the conditions that have to be present for a transaction to occur is a pretty powerful exercise. So it's all about starting with the end in mind. And you'll see in lesson three how the way we've traditionally defined a buyer might actually be sabotaging our sales and scaring off would be customers. So that's module two at a glance. By the end of this module, I want you to have a clearer, more intimate and vivid picture of your avatar, which will then allow you to connect more deeply with your messaging and generate those sacred, wow, he or she gets me moments. You're also going to have a full understanding of your prospect starting point or their initial context as it relates to your product and the pain it solves. You will get a picture of what they'll look like at the end of the journey. What do they look like as a successful, empowered customer? What do they believe? How do they feel? What is their attitude around your product, around your market, around competitors? So you're going to take a look at that, the anatomy of a buyer. And finally, you're going to connect those two points, point A and point buyer and know the seven milestones that you need to hit along the way. And armed with these insights will then be ready to leverage the five core subsequences, the segmentation, the confirmation, the pre-launch, the launch, the sales and the closing to deliver the highly tailored and focused messaging that moves your prospect through those milestones and finally onto being a customer. So when you're ready, take out your notebooks, your Evernote folder, wherever you're organizing your notes for this course and meet me in the lesson one, where we will go deeper into the buyer's journey. I'll see you there.
